questions.question: Who were the East India Company?
questions.answer: The East India Company was a British trading company that came to India for trade.

questions.question: Why did the East India Company come to India?
questions.answer: The Company came to India to trade in spices, cotton, silk and other goods.

questions.question: Who was the Nawab of Bengal defeated in the Battle of Plassey?
questions.answer: Siraj-ud-Daulah was the Nawab defeated in the Battle of Plassey.

questions.question: In which year was the Battle of Plassey fought?
questions.answer: The Battle of Plassey was fought in 1757.

questions.question: Who led the British forces in the Battle of Plassey?
questions.answer: Robert Clive led the British forces.

questions.question: What was the Battle of Buxar?
questions.answer: It was a battle fought in 1764 between the East India Company and Indian rulers.

questions.question: In which year was the Battle of Buxar fought?
questions.answer: The Battle of Buxar was fought in 1764.

questions.question: What was the Diwani?
questions.answer: Diwani was the right to collect land revenue in Bengal, Bihar and Orissa.

questions.question: Who granted the Diwani to the East India Company?
questions.answer: The Mughal emperor Shah Alam II granted the Diwani.

questions.question: What was the main source of income for the Company after Diwani?
questions.answer: Land revenue became the main source of income.

questions.question: Who introduced the Subsidiary Alliance system?
questions.answer: Lord Wellesley introduced the Subsidiary Alliance.

questions.question: What was the Subsidiary Alliance?
questions.answer: It was a system where Indian rulers accepted British troops and protection.

questions.question: Who introduced the Doctrine of Lapse?
questions.answer: Lord Dalhousie introduced the Doctrine of Lapse.

questions.question: What was the Doctrine of Lapse?
questions.answer: It was a policy allowing the British to annex states without a natural heir.

questions.question: Name one Indian state annexed under the Doctrine of Lapse.
questions.answer: Jhansi was annexed under the Doctrine of Lapse.

questions.question: Who was the ruler of Mysore who fought the British?
questions.answer: Tipu Sultan fought the British.

questions.question: Who was the father of Tipu Sultan?
questions.answer: Hyder Ali was the father of Tipu Sultan.

questions.question: Which Indian power fought several wars with the British in western India?
questions.answer: The Marathas fought several wars with the British.

questions.question: What were the Anglo-Maratha wars?
questions.answer: They were wars fought between the British and the Marathas.

questions.question: Why did the British expand their territories in India?
questions.answer: They expanded territories to gain political power and economic resources.

questions.question: What was the main aim of the East India Company?
questions.answer: The main aim was to make profit through trade.

questions.question: What helped the Company become powerful in India?
questions.answer: Military victories and political alliances helped the Company become powerful.

questions.question: What happened after the Battle of Buxar?
questions.answer: The Company gained control over revenue collection in Bengal.

questions.question: How did the Company change from traders to rulers?
questions.answer: The Company used wars, alliances and revenue control to become rulers.

questions.question: What was the result of British expansion in India?
questions.answer: British expansion led to the establishment of colonial rule.
